mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the effective federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate was cut to 1.0% in October 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) decline by more than 200,000 in a single month, it signals a sharp contraction in aggregate income and demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reset lower.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2008 payrolls fell 240000 indicating severe labor market deterioration
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines exceed 200000 during any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a broad-based contraction spanning both final demand and manufacturing output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that are larger than either signal alone.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and industrial production both declined in September 2008
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and INDPRO both declining does not produce lower average 20-day returns than the average of the two single-series signals over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In recessionary periods defined by unemployment above 6%, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) predict positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because they signal early stabilization of the cyclical component of aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment reached 6.5% in October 2008 while housing starts remained depressed
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2%, further 25 basis point cuts trigger a rapid repricing of near-term liquidity risk, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term funding pressures ease.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate was near 1.0% in October 2008 after emergency cuts
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered events over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with a concurrent month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) squeezes corporate real margins and household purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because input costs rise while demand weakens.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2008 data showed rising unemployment alongside elevated consumer price inflation
failure_condition: Joint increases in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL do not produce negative 20-day returns significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1.0% signals a severe contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days via downward earnings revisions in the industrial and materials sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2008 industrial production fell sharply month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of months when INDPRO declines exceed 1.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) larger than 1.5% triggers an abrupt repricing of consumer discretionary earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as near-term GDP forecasts are marked down.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2008 retail sales data showed severe weakness
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS declines greater than 1.5% are not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
